nodes.name: Sticky Note5

parameters.content:
## Step 6. Find Groups of Similar Answers For Each Question
[Learn more about using the Code Node]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

Giving all the responses to an LLM to analyse is the common but naive approach; the summarisation is usually too high level for real insights and loses a lot of detail such as the number and identity of respondants. What we want to do instead is find and group popular answers for each question to ensure all perspectives are considered.

Our approach does this by mapping our answer vectors to a 2D grid and then identifying where the vector points are "clustered"; where a group of points are within close proximity to each other.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note6

parameters.content:
## Step 7b. Skip If No Clusters Found
Where no clusters were found, it means the answers were unique enough to not show any pattern. eg. "What's you name?"

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note7

parameters.content:
## Step 7a. Summarise the Top Groups of Similar Answers
[Read more about using the Information Extractor Node]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

Each discovered cluster will return a reference vector which is used to fetch all related answers in the group.
The group is then sent to the LLM to summarise as well as assign a sentiment score.

the Insights Sheet we created earlier in the workflow.

nodes.name: Has Clusters?
nodes.notes: This if node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note10

parameters.content:
## Try It Out!

### This workflow generates highly-detailed insights from survey responses. Works best when dealing with a large number of participants.

* Import survey responses and vectorise in Qdrant vectorstore.
* Identify clusters of popular responses to questions using K-means clustering algorithm. 
* Each valid cluster is analysed and summarised by LLM.
* Export LLM response and cluster results back into sheet.

Check out the reference google sheet here: {{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

### Need Help?
Join the [Discord]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} or ask in the [Forum]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

Happy Hacking!

nodes.name: Apply K-means Clustering Algorithm

parameters.pythonCode:
import numpy as np
from sklearn.cluster import KMeans

# get vectors for all answers
point_ids = [item.id for item in _input.first().json.result.points]
vectors = [item.vector.to_py() for item in _input.first().json.result.points]
vectors_array = np.array(vectors)

# apply k-means clustering where n_clusters = 10
# this is a max and we'll discard some of these clusters later
kmeans = KMeans(n_clusters=min(len(vectors), 10), random_state=42).fit(vectors_array)
labels = kmeans.labels_
unique_labels = set(labels)

# Extract and print points in each cluster
clusters = {}
for label in set(labels):
    clusters[label] = vectors_array[labels == label]

# return Qdrant point ids for each cluster
# we'll use these ids to fetch the payloads from the vector store.
output = []
for cluster_id, cluster_points in clusters.items():
    points = [point_ids[i] for i in range(len(labels)) if labels[i] == cluster_id]
    output.append({
        "id": f"Cluster {cluster_id}",
        "total": len(cluster_points),
        "points": points
    })

return {"json": {"output": output } }

nodes.notes: This code node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Survey Insights Agent

options.systemPromptTemplate:
=You help summarise a selection of participant responses to a specific question for a survey called "{{ $json.result[0].payload.metadata.survey }}".
The question asked was "{{ $json.result[0].payload.metadata.question }}".
The {{ $json.result.length }} participant responses were selected because their answers were similar in context.

Your task is to: 
* summarise the given participant responses into a short paragraph. Provide an insight from this summary and what we could learn from the answers.
* determine if the overall sentiment of all the listed responses to be either negative, mildy negative, neutral, mildy positive or positive.

parameters.pythonCode:
import numpy as np
from sklearn.cluster import KMeans

# get vectors for all answers
point_ids = [item.id for item in _input.first().json.result.points]
vectors = [item.vector.to_py() for item in _input.first().json.result.points]
vectors_array = np.array(vectors)

# apply k-means clustering where n_clusters = 10
# this is a max and we'll discard some of these clusters later
kmeans = KMeans(n_clusters=min(len(vectors), 10), random_state=42).fit(vectors_array)
labels = kmeans.labels_
unique_labels = set(labels)

# Extract and print points in each cluster
clusters = {}
for label in set(labels):
    clusters[label] = vectors_array[labels == label]

# return Qdrant point ids for each cluster
# we'll use these ids to fetch the payloads from the vector store.
output = []
for cluster_id, cluster_points in clusters.items():
    points = [point_ids[i] for i in range(len(labels)) if labels[i] == cluster_id]
    output.append({
        "id": f"Cluster {cluster_id}",
        "total": len(cluster_points),
        "points": points
    })

return {"json": {"output": output } }

parameters.content:
## Try It Out!

### This workflow generates highly-detailed insights from survey responses. Works best when dealing with a large number of participants.

* Import survey responses and vectorise in Qdrant vectorstore.
* Identify clusters of popular responses to questions using K-means clustering algorithm. 
* Each valid cluster is analysed and summarised by LLM.
* Export LLM response and cluster results back into sheet.

Check out the reference google sheet here: {{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

### Need Help?
Join the [Discord]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} or ask in the [Forum]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

Happy Hacking!

### First Time Running?
There is a slight delay on first run because the code node has to download the required packages.

## Step 6. Find Groups of Similar Answers For Each Question
[Learn more about using the Code Node]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

Giving all the responses to an LLM to analyse is the common but naive approach; the summarisation is usually too high level for real insights and loses a lot of detail such as the number and identity of respondants. What we want to do instead is find and group popular answers for each question to ensure all perspectives are considered.

Our approach does this by mapping our answer vectors to a 2D grid and then identifying where the vector points are "clustered"; where a group of points are within close proximity to each other.

## Step 7b. Skip If No Clusters Found
Where no clusters were found, it means the answers were unique enough to not show any pattern. eg. "What's you name?"

## Step 7a. Summarise the Top Groups of Similar Answers
[Read more about using the Information Extractor Node]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

Each discovered cluster will return a reference vector which is used to fetch all related answers in the group.
The group is then sent to the LLM to summarise as well as assign a sentiment score.

## Step 8. Write To Insights Sheet
Finally, our completed insights to appended to
the Insights Sheet we created earlier in the workflow.

### Run this once! 
If for any reason you need to run more than once, be sure to clear the existing data first.

testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).

instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
